intro: Ammi visnaga paste, also known as sea parsley or khella, is a natural remedy derived from the seeds of the Ammi visnaga plant, a member of the carrot family. Traditionally used in Middle Eastern and North African medicine, the paste is valued for its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Its use is also linked to the management of edema and other cardiovascular conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in these areas.
side_effects: Ammi visnaga paste, also known as khella, is derived from the plant Ammi visnaga and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. However, its long-term use or excessive consumption may lead to adverse effects. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health side effects of ammi visnaga paste.
uses: Ammi visnaga paste is used to help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. It is also used as a natural remedy to treat gastrointestinal issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and stomach ulcers. recipe: To make ammi visnaga paste for medicinal use, clean and dry the roots of the plant, then grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Mix the powder with a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, in a 1:10 ratio to create a smooth paste. Store the paste in an airtight container in a cool, dark place to preserve its medicinal properties.
taste: Ammi visnaga paste has a unique and somewhat bitter taste, often described as earthy and slightly sweet. The flavor profile is often compared to celery or parsley, but with a more intense and herbaceous note. The bitterness is due to the presence of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its distinct taste and aroma.
